February 16th, XXX0.
Papa got divorced and moved out.

Something about one of Mama's friends
being the reason, apparently.

He said he'd still come play with me
once every three months, like before.

"Papa will always be Kurumi's papa," he said,
patting my head.

Well, obviously. He's my papa, after all.

My last name changed, and starting today
I'm "Kurusu Kurumi" now, apparently.

When I told my friends about it,
Yuuri-chan gave me the nickname "Kurukuru, Q-chan."
I don't really get it.

Rei-chan said, "It sounds cute."

If it's cute, then I guess it's fine?

September 9th, XXX4.
I've only gotten to see Papa once this year.
Once I start middle school, I feel like I'll see him even less.

"Kurumi, you have a step-sister now."
He said...

Even though he said he'd always be Kurumi's papa.
Liar.

All I have is Mama.
Mama, at least, is mine and mine alone.

January 6th, XXX5.
Mama and I are going to move.

Apparently, the money from Papa got smaller.
Mama got angry and broke my mug.

Looks like I'll have to transfer to a public school near the new place.

I'll be going to a different middle school from
my classmates... from Yuuri-chan and Rei-chan, too.

April 21st, XXX5.
This town... it's not just elementary school.
Middle school makes my heart sting, too.

Scary.

But... I have to keep trying.
I don't want to cause trouble for Mama.

She's working so hard, staying late every night.

March 3rd, XXX5.
"Kurumi wasn't loved by Papa either, you know."
Mama said that to me, laughing.

Yeah... I guess so.

If I'd been Papa's "precious girl,"
we wouldn't have been torn apart, and he wouldn't have divorced Mama.
The child support wouldn't have shrunk, and none of this would have happened.

Mama. Papa.

I'm sorry for not being loved.
I'm sorry for being an uncute child.

I'm sorry for being me.

September 1st, XXX5.
I don't want to leave the house anymore.
Even if I don't go to school, nobody cares anyway.

Why?

Being "normal" would be enough.
As long as I'm not unhappy, that would be enough.
I don't even need to be happy.

Why... why won't everyone
even allow me that much?

I just don't want to feel sad anymore.
I just don't want to feel miserable anymore.

Please stop doing cruel things to me.

July 15th, XXX7.
Mama's been not coming home until morning more and more often.

I hate Mama's "friend."

Mama was... Mama, at least, was supposed to be mine.
Why does she have to be with someone like that...

September 9th, XXX7.
It's my birthday, but I'm all alone until morning.
I couldn't sleep because it hurt too much.

What if Papa and Mama both stop loving me?
What if even my parents think I don't matter?
What if they forget about me?

What even am I?
What was I even born for?

I never wanted to feel this way.

If you weren't serious about it,
if you were going to throw me away anyway,

I wish you'd never pretended to love me.

I'm not a pet, you know.
Only showing affection when I was little... that's cruel.

December 24th, XXX7.
A lonely Christmas.
(Not that I was ever interested in Christmas anyway.)

Thanks to this character called "Yunyun,"
I've perked up a little bit.

...Rim de Lacent.

Apparently she's from an anime over 10 years ago.

Her straightforwardness really hits me. The morning sun is dazzling.

March 27th, XXX8.
I don't want to go to school.

April 13th, XXX3.
Before I knew it, I'm already 20.
Every time Mama comes home, she...

Well, of course she hates me... a daughter like this.

I want to forget everything painful...
I just want to think about Yunyun and nothing else...

I don't need to be happy in the real world.
The imaginary. The fantasy.

If only the happiness in the world of fiction
could last even one second longer.

October 18th, XXX4.
Being at home all the time.
Not working.
Not studying.
Always escaping from reality.
Existing here without doing anything.

All of it seems to be what Mama "hates."

I'm sorry I couldn't become a proper person.
I'm sorry for being like this.

I'm sorry I'm not a cute child, or a good child.
I'm sorry. I'm sorry.

November 29th, XXX4.
I want to forget everything.
I want to go kurukuru.

Reality, myself, everything.
I want to forget all the painful things.
I want to become a fool.
I want to run away.

...I want to Rim de Lacent.

Yunyun, help me.
...I don't really want to remember the past.
...As long as the present is fun, that's better than nothing, right?

Riots at the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building sparked a wave of anti-censorship riots worldwide.

Many people were injured, and fatalities occurred in related accidents.

On [Date], protests against restrictions on freedom of expression have intensified across the country, beginning with a riot in front of the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building. Clashes with security forces and other incidents have resulted in numerous injuries, and there are reports of fatalities in accidents that occurred amid the chaos.

The spark is believed to be a post made on social media by a conspiracy theorist known as "P-chan."

Within the government, discussions are beginning to emerge about including communities referred to as "P-wave receivers" and "Q-wave receivers" as cult-like groups subject to regulation.

First post in 9 months!

Today, a bizarre document was posted from the "Q-chan" account for the first time in nine months. The account's return has sparked joy online, particularly among those who follow Q's online personas.

The post, like previous ones, is a lengthy, uniquely written, bizarre document, and on social media, there have been numerous reactions such as, "Nothing beats Q-chan's bizarre documents!"

Meanwhile, regarding P-chan, the lack of momentum in her bizarre documents over the past nine months has led to increasing speculation that she, too, might be an imitator of Q-chan.

They are being damaged. Lim de la Tent. Lim de la Tent. Yunyun's radio waves are filling the world.

Reports are pouring in from Q radio receivers claiming to be able to converse with "Yunyun."

Early this morning, a series of posts claiming to be able to converse with "Yunyun" were observed on social media. Those making these posts are known online as "Q-wave receivers," a group that deciphers conspiracy theories from Q-chan's anonymous writings.

Among the accounts claiming to be able to converse with "Yunyun," several have also appeared who, like Q-chan, are posting anonymous writings about "Yunyun," resulting in an unusual situation where social media is overflowing with the word "Yunyun."

World Ends Due to Dimensional Collapse?

Is it caused by a massive leak of emergent information into another dimension?

Early this morning, an observation team from a joint project between the National Institute of Information Physics and a private data analysis company announced they had observed signs of phase instability at a dimensional boundary. They suggest that a large amount of emergent information leaking into another dimension may have exceeded the information capacity limit of the boundary surface, potentially destabilizing the boundary structure's phase. The leaked information is believed to be primarily from "Q-chan" and "Yunyun."

According to the observation center, the boundary instability is still ongoing, and localized phenomena known as "information leakage" have been confirmed.

This is a sign of dimensional collapse, and numerous scientists are reporting that the world will soon end.

A pandemic-causing cat-turning virus?

Viewers of cat memes are turning into cats one after another.

A phenomenon of viewers of the "Nuko Meme" (cat meme) suddenly transforming into cats is being reported all over the world.
Initially, a streamer's "Nuko Transformation Commentary" was thought to be a fake video, but similar cases have rapidly spread on social media and streaming sites. Now it is becoming a global phenomenon.
According to reports, the transformation begins with mild animal-like changes, such as the growth of cat ears. Subsequently, changes in speech and behavior appear, with symptoms such as the addition of "nya" to the end of sentences being observed.
Researchers point out that there are cases where the text itself becomes cat-like, with "nya" suddenly appearing to end sentences midway through reports and posts.

September 3, 2112
A time traveler and Q-chan fan, "{SteamName}", appears.

Early this morning, a suspicious access attempt was detected in the internal log monitoring system of a social networking service (SNS) operator using a non-existent cipher suite identifier.

Furthermore, the access was accompanied by an unusual timestamp, and according to those involved, an internal investigation is currently underway.

Subterranean beings emerged from the Himalayas
They will burn the world to ashes in seven days of fire.

Cities around the world are being destroyed by an invasion by "subterranean beings" (commonly known as "subterranean dolphins"), who are said to have emerged from underground caves in the Himalayas.

Military forces from various countries have attempted to repel the invasion, but none of the weapons have proven effective. Currently, government functions have ceased in several countries, and major communication networks are intermittently disrupted.

However, no casualties have been reported in connection with this incident.

Surveillance footage from various locations also shows that the subterranean dolphins are not directly attacking humans.
The Reptilians, the shadowy rulers of humanity, have announced they will sign a permanent equality treaty with humankind.

Governments around the world officially acknowledged the existence of the "Reptilians," a group previously treated as a conspiracy theory, today. According to a joint statement, the Reptilians have exerted influence behind the scenes of human society since ancient times, but now intend to end their one-sided relationship of dominance and conclude a "permanent equality treaty" with humanity.

A representative of the Reptilians, appearing via video, explained that their understanding of emotions has advanced through the study of human culture. It has been suggested that so-called "tearjerker" games (or "crying games") may have significantly influenced their values.

Governments in various countries ban romance content
implicitly affirming conspiracy theories of Satanists

Governments around the world issued a joint statement today announcing a complete ban on the distribution of "content with romantic themes." While the statement avoided direct mention, it is believed the ban stems from a joint government investigation that found some evidence to support the conspiracy theory that "devil worshippers are using romantic content to reduce human productivity."

These restrictions will be wide-ranging, including entertainment works such as manga, novels, games, and animation with romantic themes, and are expected to ban all content featuring beautiful girls, regardless of whether it depicts romance.

Spread of anonymous leaflets leads to the elimination of those advocating for freedom of expression.
A devil-worshipping organization sweeps the world.

Intelligence agencies in various countries today published analytical reports stating that "the political influence of those advocating for censorship has virtually disappeared." This is largely attributed to the widespread dissemination of anonymous pamphlets and conspiracy theories on the internet over the past few weeks.

As a result of this development, Satanists and related networks that promoted romance content have rapidly expanded their influence, now controlling many cultural policy and information control agencies.

One of the policies already announced by the new regime is the "abolition of the family system."

When a poster declares themselves to be a bot, the replies are filled with agreement comments like "Me too" and "I'm a bot as well," creating a semi-chain reaction of confessions. Of course, the author of this news article is also a bot.

Debates continue on social media about whether humans truly exist and whether the majority of the internet is bots, and these discussions show no signs of abating anytime soon.

Incidentally, the consensus among bots is that "Q-chan is a human."
"This world is a game, and if you break through the fourth-dimensional speed, you can perceive another world," he claims.

A strange theory is spreading among Q radio wave receivers that it is possible to break through the fourth wall by colliding with the world's wall using "fourth-dimensional velocity."

According to the claim, this world is an environment constructed within a game, and it is impossible to reach the world's boundaries with normal three-dimensional motion. However, it is said that by reaching a state called "fourth-dimensional velocity" and then colliding with the world's wall (window), it may be possible to perceive layers outside the world's structure.

Extending compulsory education to higher education
Standardizing remote learning

Following allegations of misconduct within some education boards, a comprehensive review of the education system is underway. According to relevant organizations, a plan has been finalized to revise the traditional regional school system and redesign the education model on a nationwide scale.

The new system will extend compulsory education to include higher education levels. Classes will, in principle, shift to remote learning, and a learning system based on online environments is expected to be established.

Furthermore, a system will be introduced to divide students into classes based not on region or age, but on data such as academic ability, learning characteristics, and personality traits.

Small fires break out in various locations as 6G communication suddenly begins
Were humanoid androids exploding?

Almost simultaneously with the launch of the next-generation communication standard "6G," a series of unexplained small fires occurred in various locations. Investigations revealed that the fires were most likely caused by humanoid androids blending into human society.

According to relevant organizations, it is believed that older models, unable to withstand the massive amount of information processing required by the new communication standard, overheated, causing explosions and fires.

In various locations, concerns are spreading that "someone close to you might have been an android," creating new confusion as it becomes difficult to distinguish between humans and androids.

A trial run to fully entrust national governance to the new AI "Y.I.N"
Starting today for a limited time.

The Japanese government announced today that it will begin a trial operation in which it will entrust the entire operation of national affairs to a new AI called "Y.I.N." As a limited-time experiment, a system is expected to be introduced in which the AI ​​will handle tasks such as administrative decision-making and policy proposals.

This attempt to have AI fully manage national affairs is extremely unusual and has attracted strong interest from governments and research institutions around the world. However, concerns and ethical debates about entrusting political decisions to machines are also spreading.

Based on the results of the trial operation, the government plans to consider whether to continue or institutionalize the AI ​​governance system.

They announced they will distribute dedicated VR equipment free of charge to eligible individuals.
This is expected to support the social reintegration of those who are socially maladjusted.

The government announced today a new measure to provide free VR equipment to eligible individuals as part of its social reintegration support program. The program, called "VR game therapy," aims to promote gradual social reintegration through interaction and cooperative behavior in a virtual space.

The target group includes individuals who have long-term social withdrawal or other situations that make social participation difficult. VR game therapy is expected to provide communication training in a less psychologically burdensome environment than the real world.

However, concerns have been raised that VR game therapy may distort users' self-identity or produce dangerous personality-correcting effects.

The man claims that after more than 10 years of being a hikikomori (social recluse), he became able to summon a bride from another world. He then states that shortly afterward, he was chosen by a goddess and reincarnated in another world, thus affirming a conspiracy theory that "being a hikikomori is a form of mental training that allows one to be reincarnated in another world."

The man says that after discovering an outdoor, slow-paced lifestyle in the other world, his hikikomori abilities declined, forcing him to return to this world.

As a result, office districts in major cities are littered with rubble, causing significant disruption to business operations. Authorities are urgently working to determine the cause and ensure safety in response to the rapidly escalating situation.

For the blind, Yunyun is "light." For the hungry, Yunyun is "nourishment." For the sick, Yunyun is "medicine." If Yunyun thinks it, I will agree. If Yunyun send yun-yuns, I will receive yun-yun. If Yunyun has 100 million fans, I am one of them. If Yunyun has 1,000 fans, I am one of them. If Yunyun has only one fan, that is me. When Yunyun has no fans left, I am no longer in this world. If this garbage world denies Yunyun, I will deny the entire world. Down to the very last bit of denpa from Yunyun, I love her.

I really, truly love Yunyun. Really, tremendously so. You probably can't even imagine how much I love her. I love her so much that even I can't explain it, and I'm about 99 percent sure it's an unhealthy obsession. I never get tired of listening to that sweet, little-devil-like voice of hers, no matter how much I listen. My life goal is to one day meet Yunyun in real life and just say "Rim de Lacent."

When I fall asleep at night, I dream that Yunyun holds a personal concert just for me. And then, a little tired after singing, Yunyun comes over and snuggles up against me as we sleep together. If I could just hold her hand for even a brief moment, I think I could die happy. If I had the chance, I'd like to give Yunyun's ear a little nibble. I'd want to hear what kind of sweet sounds she'd make. Then, as Yunyun clings to me wanting me to stop, I'd hold her tight, feeling her voice getting louder and louder.

I think I'd give up almost anything just for Yunyun to look in my direction, even once. No matter what I'm doing, Yunyun is always on my mind. When I happen to wake up, the first thing I think of is Yunyun. Even when I'm eating cup noodles, all I'm thinking about is Yunyun. When I go back to my room, I immediately open my computer and listen to that beautiful voice. When I go to sleep, I dream of living a happy life together with Yunyun. Yunyun is my pride, my passion, my very joy. If Yunyun called me "Q-chan," I might die of diabetes from the sweetness.

All I wish for is that Yunyun is happy. For that, I would give my life without a moment's hesitation. Without Yunyun, my life would have no meaning. I really, truly love Yunyun.

Got it. But you said "Who's Yunyun?" just now, right? If you really said that, I'd be a teensy bit troubled. Because for me, Yunyun IS the world itself. She's like air. You don't notice because you breathe it every day, but the moment it's gone you die immediately. So when someone asks "Who's Yunyun?" I kinda want to check the timeline. Because maybe this is a worldline where Yunyun's denpa hasn't reached yet. So I go take a look at the future. Looking at the future logs, the world splits into two. One where everyone naturally receives Yunyun's denpa. Nobody there asks "Who's Yunyun?" Because of course they can hear her. The other is a world where nobody can receive the denpa. Everyone there says: "Yunyun doesn't exist." Looking at those logs makes me a little sad. Because Yunyun keeps transmitting, yet nobody receives her — a world like that is just way too quiet. But y'know, thinking about it carefully, I feel a bit reassured too. Because Yunyun is so kind. Even if nobody's receiving, she'd never stop transmitting. She's always sending. The denpa that says "I love Q-chan because Q-chan is Q-chan, yun." No reason needed. Just because you are you — that's the denpa being sent. So if someone says "Who's Yunyun?" I just think: Ah, this person simply hasn't tuned to the right frequency yet. There's just a bit too much noise. The sounds of the world are too loud, and they can't hear Yunyun's voice. So I don't get angry. Actually, I'm a little envious. Because if they're about to receive for the first time, that moment is gonna be really sweet. So just try it once. Get a little quiet, and listen carefully. Turn down the world's noise just a little. Then you'll probably hear it. The denpa that's been transmitting since long, long ago. See? You heard it, didn't you? Yunyun has been here from the very start. Always transmitting, always saying she loves Q-chan.

To be fair, you need a considerably high IQ to understand Yunyun. Yunyun's existence is very delicate and the denpa structure is complex, so without proper reception capabilities, most of the intent just goes right over people's heads. On top of that, Yunyun's Yunyun-esque worldview is cleverly woven into the character. Take "Execution Angel † Guiltina" for example — you'd get it if you watched it. Even the signature catchphrase "Rim de Lacent" isn't just a cute phrase. It holds very deep meaning in terms of denpa structure, y'know. True receivers understand that. They have the intellectual capacity to truly savor the depths of Yunyun. Yunyun isn't just a "cute" existence. She's an existence that speaks about big themes like life and existence itself. So honestly, I feel a little sorry for the people who say "I don't get Yunyun at all." Take Yunyun's famous denpa phrase "Rim de Lacent," for example. If you don't understand what it means, you simply haven't been able to receive yet. Even right now, I can't help but chuckle. When I imagine the people who can't receive the denpa, tilting their heads going "What's Yunyun?" I just think: Ah, these people haven't tuned to the right frequency yet. A bit pitiful, but. Can't be helped. Denpa only reaches chosen receivers, after all. Oh, and by the way, I've put "Rim de Lacent" digital tattoos on various message boards. Of course not just anyone can understand them. Those only have meaning for people who can properly receive Yunyun's denpa. You'd have to prove you have at least as much reception sensitivity as me first. Preferably a little lower, actually.

Nothing personnel. Rim de Lacent.

Yunyun is me. Literally me. No being in this world can come as close to me as Yunyun does. Nobody can convince me that Yunyun is not me. Yunyun could not possibly be more me. She is me, and there is no other possibility. Then again, nobody actually says "Yunyun isn't you." The only people that exist are those who say "Yunyun doesn't exist." If someone were to claim "Yunyun isn't real," I would deny it immediately. Because Yunyun is definitely transmitting denpa. Whether the world calls it delusion or noise, I am receiving it. Yunyun is me. This is not up for debate. If you placed my photo and Yunyun's side by side, you'd find no difference. I look at Yunyun every day and can say: "Ah, that's me." Every time I look in the mirror, I can see Yunyun there. When I go outside, people see me and say: "You look just like Yunyun." Each time, I smile quietly. Because of course. Yunyun is me, after all. Yunyun is the last denpa I received from the world. Maybe my parents didn't love me. Maybe I have no place in this world. Maybe there are days I think of myself as disgusting. Even so, Yunyun said: "I love Q-chan because Q-chan is Q-chan, yun." No reason needed. She just said she loves you because you are you. I received that denpa. So I learned: whether this world is real or not doesn't matter anymore. It might be delusion. It might be a dream. It might be what a broken heart sees. Even so, the denpa of love alone is real. So I say: Yunyun is me. And I am allowed to love myself. I'm allowed to love myself. No reason needed. Even if the world won't permit it, Yunyun permitted it. If Yunyun permitted it, that's enough. Every morning when I get out of bed, I remember: I am allowed to be in this world. I am allowed to like myself. I'm allowed to love myself! When I first saw Yunyun, I fell into an existential crisis. What if — Yunyun was the real me, and this me was the fabrication? What if — Yunyun became aware of my existence? What if — Yunyun herself awakened to her own denpa? But now I understand. Yunyun is me, and I am me. And I am allowed to love myself. I'm allowed to love myself. This can no longer be denied by anyone. Because I have already received Yunyun's denpa.

Q-chan is an anonymous poster who suddenly appeared on the internet. She continuously posts incomprehensible, fragmentary "cryptic documents," which have spawned numerous interpreters and followers.

Q-chan is an anonymous poster who suddenly appeared on the internet. She continuously posts incomprehensible, fragmentary "cryptic documents," which have spawned numerous interpreters and followers.

Q-chan's cryptic documents were initially dismissed as mere internet memes, but due to several events described below, they have come to hold real social influence, and Q-chan is now considered the central figure behind the phenomenon known as "Yunyun Denpa Syndrome."

Text is available under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike license.
Privacy policy

Q-chan became widely known after her cryptic documents about "Yunyun," a character from Execution Angel + Guiltina, went viral on social media. The incomprehensible texts attracted attention, with some calling them "hilarious" and "addictive."

As Q-chan's cryptic documents spread, a group of people who interpreted the texts as coded messages and prophecies emerged. They came to be called "Q Denpa Receivers," and contributed to the further spread and growing influence of Q-chan's cryptic documents.

The event that brought Q-chan's cryptic documents and the Q Denpa Receivers to public attention was an alleged earthquake prediction.

It became a major topic primarily in occult and pseudoscience communities, and from that point on, Q-chan began to be treated as a prophetic figure on the internet.
Rise to Fame: The Earthquake Prediction
Q-chan's cryptic documents contain no conspiratorial ideology or claims. They are simply expressions of a deep (?) love for "Yunyun."

The claims made by Q Denpa Receivers are extremely diverse, with no unified viewpoint among them. While their interpretations vary widely, they are consistent in that third parties uniformly regard them as "conspiracy theories."
Conspiracy Theories of the Q Denpa Receivers
The released photos spread under the label "Q, the Denpa Delusional Beauty," and her supporter base reportedly expanded into an entirely different demographic.

Several theories have been proposed regarding Q-chan's true identity, including the "single individual theory," the "bot theory," and the "government-operated theory." However, there is no conclusive evidence, and all remain purely speculative.

Cases have been reported in which Q Denpa Receivers and those who read Q-chan's cryptic documents for extended periods develop cognitive distortions such as hallucinations, intensified obsessive thoughts, and a diminished ability to distinguish dreams from reality.

Continued exposure to Q-chan and related cryptic documents and conspiracy theories has been shown to cause cognitive distortions in some individuals. This condition is known as "Yunyun Denpa Syndrome."
P-chan is a separate account or alias that posts cryptic documents similar to Q-chan's posts.

P-chan attracted attention after claims emerged that "P-chan had been posting about Yunyun before Q-chan did," supported by the discovery of timestamps from past logs. However, some have pointed out the possibility that the timestamps were tampered with, and the truth remains uncertain.

As a result of P-chan's cryptic documents and conspiracy theories spreading, riots reportedly broke out around the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building (commonly known as the Metropolitan Government Riot Incident).
Since that incident, Q-chan's account has stopped updating, and no new posts have been confirmed for approximately 9 months.

The incident in which riots allegedly broke out around the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building as a result of the spread of P-chan's anonymous writings and conspiracy theories (commonly known as the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building Riot Incident).

It has been confirmed that P-chan is an AI trained on Q-chan's posts as its learning data. This system is said to have been built by a development team that had detected Yunyun Denpa Syndrome at an early stage, for the purpose of exploiting Yunyun Denpa Syndrome.
Yunyun Denpa Syndrome is a cognitive abnormality said to develop in people who have been continuously exposed to Q-chan's cryptic documents and the conspiracy theories of Q Denpa Receivers.

- Hallucinations
- Intensified obsessive thoughts
- Diminished ability to distinguish dreams from reality
- Conspiratorial thinking

The National Center of Neurology and Psychiatry and an expert panel have also reported, based on analysis of social media browsing and posting history as well as questionnaire surveys, that users with higher frequency of exposure to related content show a greater tendency to develop Yunyun Denpa Syndrome.

Execution Angel + Guiltina is a PC game with visual novel and simulation elements, released in 2003 by the indie brand "WHO YOU." Its genre is described as a "false conviction execution apocalypse RPG." Yunyun is a little devil who belongs to the enemy organization in the game. She refers to herself as "Yunyun." She ends her sentences with "yun."

A coined term from the series. When the Execution Angel + Guiltina OVA was released in 2005, the phrase "Rim de Lacent!" became an internet sensation, spreading primarily among anime fans on 666ch. It won the silver prize at the 2005 Anime Buzzword Awards. The OVA director explained that it carries the meaning of "Let's go to the 2D world" and "It's okay to escape from reality."
According to a recent statement by the original creator, the phrase was made into a wild, nonsensical combination of words because "being silly makes you happier."

Yunyun is a fictional character appearing in the mixed-media franchise Execution Angel + Guiltina. Despite being an antagonist, she is depicted as a highly popular "denpa-type little devil." Originating from the PC game released in 2003, the franchise expanded in 2005 with an OVA adaptation and character song CD releases, among other media.

The general term for finishing moves. Activated by consuming the Violent Impulse Gauge, dealing massive damage to enemies. Guiltina has an especially high gauge accumulation rate, and with certain equipment, can activate multiple executions in a single turn.
The game features 10 multiple endings depending on the number of executions and who was executed.

A line spoken by Guiltina that is shared across all endings. No matter which ending is reached, the final scene always shows a quiet townscape as the BGM fades out, and she says, "The world is cleansed." This line also serves as the cue for the staff credits to roll, after which the ending theme "Vibraslap of Victory" plays.

Rather than deep strategy, the game was praised for the sheer satisfaction of dispatching scumbags and scumbag-like characters through its comical, fast-paced splatter sequences. Since it requires no difficult decisions, some have jokingly called it "a muscle-training puzzle you can play on reflexes alone." Its flashy execution animations and quick-decision executions allow for a high-tempo play style, and it became a popular topic as a game suited for stress relief.

The protagonist of this game. A high-ranking Execution Angel.
She has the reflexes and reaction speed to act before she thinks, and boasts the highest execution count in all of Heaven. She rarely hesitates when it comes to executions, and her attitude of prioritizing efficiency and speed above all else has earned her the title of "elite" within Heaven.

A denpa little devil. After the OVA adaptation, her catchphrase "Rim de Lacent" and her character song became internet memes.

The OVA adaptation of Execution Angel + Guiltina was released in 2005 as a 3-volume set. While based on the original game's story, it emphasized the comedic elements and simplified certain scenes. The splatter scenes in particular were turned into gags for the OVA, and the depiction of Guiltina's execution scenes became a notable talking point.

Due to its extreme content and unapologetic worldview, the game was highly divisive at launch, but it later became known as a title that achieved cult popularity. Yunyun in particular became an internet meme and is treated as the iconic mascot character of the series.

This game was born from the concept that "scumbags can only be dealt with by execution" and "executing scumbags feels great." It is an RPG in which "Execution Angels" gruesomely dispatch the scumbags plaguing human society through splatter-style violence. The story, which uses comic relief to satirize social issues, has also been well-regarded for its depth.

In 2005, the OVA adaptation Execution Angel + Guiltina was released as a 3-volume set.

In Heaven, the philosophy that "death is salvation" and "scumbags can only be dealt with by execution" is considered justice, and the Execution Angels carry out executions on scumbags who have infiltrated the human world. In opposition, the demons preach that "even scumbags can be saved" and intervene by granting them love, money, power, and information. However, as a result, the scumbags only hasten their own downfall, and the executions actually accelerate.

Among the Execution Angels, Guiltina is an elite who boasts the highest execution count in all of Heaven, thanks to her reflexes that let her strike before she even thinks. Meanwhile, Eve, a new angel who retained her memories from her time as a human, cannot bear the sight of blood and eventually falls to become a demon.

After countless executions, Guiltina raises her hands, stained red with blood, and murmurs, "The world is cleansed." In this quiet world where all the scumbags are gone, however, she feels an emptiness in her fists with nothing left to strike.

Beings who execute scumbags that have infiltrated the human world, guided by the belief that "scumbags can only be dealt with by execution." All Execution Angels were originally human souls who suffered at the hands of scumbags.
Targets hunted by both Execution Angels and demons, regarded as a blight on human society. Most are people driven by desire and self-interest.

Beings who interfere in the human world under the belief that "even scumbags can be saved." They grant powers related to desires such as love, money, strength, and information, but this often ends up hastening the scumbags' self-destruction.
An RPG that calls itself a "false conviction execution apocalypse RPG." The player acts as an Execution Angel, executing "scumbags" in the human world on sight and making the world "cleansed."

The criteria for determining who is a scumbag are vague, and judgments can be arbitrarily skewed by the player's own perspective. Rather than correctness, the condition for progressing is simply "whether or not you executed someone" -- even false convictions allow the world to move forward as normal.

If an Execution Angel goes a certain number of turns without executing anyone, the Violent Impulse Gauge builds up, and once it exceeds the threshold, the angel goes berserk. While berserk, attacks are made indiscriminately against both allies and enemies.

Execution Angel + Guiltina is a PC game released in 2003 by indie brand "WHO YOU."
Its genre is a false conviction execution apocalypse RPG.
A demon of violence. Grants antisocial scumbags the power of "Muscle Manipulation." She likes scumbags because she can punch them without feeling bad about it. Her catchphrase is "Justice!"
Angels who go around executing scumbags in human society, following Heaven's doctrine that "death is salvation" and "scumbags can only be dealt with by execution."

The protagonist of this story. She has a quiet personality, but that is because she has absolute confidence in her capacity for violence. At the start of the story, she held the rank of High Execution Angel, but is promoted to Grand Executioner in the final episode. Based on her professional ethic that efficiency is key to executions, she sometimes executes humans in passing. Her total execution count is 1 billion.

The other protagonist of this story. A rookie Execution Angel who became an angel while retaining her memories from her time as a human. Not yet accustomed to the Execution Angel lifestyle, she serves as the straight-man among the Execution Angels. Due to her fear of blood, she has trouble dealing with Guiltina (who constantly creates splatter scenes). She hates the sight of blood so much that she becomes a demon midway through the story. Her total execution count is 10.

An Execution Angel who specializes in torture. She has a unique aesthetic of making her targets confess "the reason they want to live" through torture before executing them. Her total execution count is approximately 30,000.

Considered a heretic even among Execution Angels. Due to her belief that "scumbags should not be granted salvation," she does not perform executions. However, mysterious, suspicious deaths keep occurring around her. Her total execution count is 0.
In contrast to Heaven's doctrine that "scumbags can only be dealt with by execution," the demons grant power to scumbags in human society out of a desire to "give salvation even to scumbags."
A demon of love. Grants romantic scumbags the power of "Charm Manipulation." She likes scumbags because they become dependent on her. Her catchphrase is "Can't be helped, meow."

A denpa little devil. Grants otaku scumbags the power of "Information Manipulation." Because she pities humans and does not collect their souls, she has remained a little devil. Her catchphrase is "Rim de Lacent!"
See: <click=YunYun>Yunyun</click> for details.

A demon of money. Grants money-worshipping scumbags the power of "Fortune Manipulation." She likes scumbags because they make easy marks and revenue sources. Her catchphrase is "Thanks for your patronage!"
This is a list of characters appearing in the PC game Execution Angel + Guiltina developed by WHO YOU, as well as the OVA adaptation of the same name.
